title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Pierce informs Hakast that Ila Brent approached him as people were leaving the trial. She had been looking for someone and asked to speak to the officer who provided evidence that day. Because Hakast was busy with the county police commissioner, Pierce advised her to leave a message or return to the station. Ila claimed her business was not important, though she appeared puzzled and remarked, "I don't know if what she said is true." Ila was later found strangled in a phone booth.

Hakast reflects on Ila's possible motives, wondering if she had seen or heard something related to Hilary Webb's testimony. Hercule Poirot investigates the possibility that Ila went to Wilbraham Crescent to visit someone. He notes three potential addresses: number 20 (Mrs. Blackham), number 19 (Miss Pembroke), and number 18 (Miss Watkins). Poirot considers that the murder weapon, a knife, was found at number 20, and wonders if Ila suspected Miss Pembroke of having a relationship with Hilary Webb or if Miss Watkins, who found the body, was involved.

Hakast calls Colin Lumb, who confirms he had lunch with Sheila Webb at a Chinese restaurant on Market Street at 1:00 PM, following her shopping trip. This contradicts the fact that Ila Brent died between 12:30 and 1:00 PM. Later, Poirot visits Miss Pembroke at 19 Wilbraham Crescent. She claims she was at home around 12:30 PM and denies that Ila Brent came to see her, expressing anger upon hearing of the murder.
